findings.text: The design does not define how Codex reasoning signatures are retained and reused across turns, even though `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requires in the scenario **"Reasoning stream with signature retention"** that the system keep "the reasoning signature needed for later turns." `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` only specifies `_session_id` passthrough via `extra_params` and generic thinking-event mapping; it never states where the returned signature is stored, what type carries it, or how later requests send it back.

The wire-level Codex contract is not frozen inside the OpenSpec artifacts. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` says the "reference implementation exists" only in external files under `/home/brittonr/git/pirs/pi-mono/...` and uses that as the source of "important wire-level expectations." But `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requires a provider-required compatibility prefix, required Codex beta/compatibility headers, and an account identifier header. The design mentions `chatgpt-account-id` and "Codex beta/compat markers" but does not enumerate the exact headers, prefix behavior, or the JWT claim used to derive the account ID. That leaves implementation dependent on an external/private oracle.

Verification is too vague for design-stage completeness. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` only has scattered statements like "cover parsing with recorded fixture tests," "`_session_id` end-to-end tests through both local and RPC provider paths," and "keep one manual smoke path for real credentials." It does not provide an explicit verification plan covering the scenarios already committed in `specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` and `specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md`, including proactive refresh, exactly one 401 refresh-and-retry, bounded 429/5xx retry behavior, provider-scoped status/switch/logout, and discovery hiding `openai-codex` when credentials are absent.

This change reintroduces a known streaming-test risk without elevating the guardrail. The 2026-04-08 napkin entry says stream rewriter changes need at least one test at the real seam `parse_sse_stream(..., reverse_map = true)`, not only helper/unit coverage. Here, `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` adds SSE normalization requirements for text, tool calls, and reasoning streams, while `design.md` only commits to unit + fixture coverage. Promote the seam-test requirement into the change’s verification rules before implementation.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requires that request headers “include the account identifier required by the Codex backend,” but `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` only specifies storing OAuth credentials and active-account state (“stores OAuth credentials under provider `openai-codex`”, “updates the persisted auth store entry for provider `openai-codex`”). No requirement states how that account identifier is obtained, persisted, refreshed, or loaded. This leaves the auth-store shape and request-header source under-specified.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/proposal.md` has `Why`, `What Changes`, `Capabilities`, and `Impact`, but no explicit non-goals or verification expectations. Given the stated impact spans `src/commands/auth.rs`, `src/slash_commands/handlers/auth.rs`, `crates/clankers-provider/src/{auth.rs,discovery.rs,lib.rs,router.rs,rpc_provider.rs}`, and `../clanker-router/src/{auth.rs,oauth.rs,provider.rs}`, the stage artifact is incomplete on boundaries and acceptance evidence.

The streaming requirements in `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` cover text, tool calls, reasoning, and usage normalization, but the change has no explicit verification requirement at the real SSE parser seam. This repeats a known failure mode from the napkin: helper-only rewrite tests missed runtime behavior, and changes to stream rewriters need at least one test at `parse_sse_stream(..., reverse_map = true)`. Because the proposal adds “a new OpenAI Codex backend module and related tests” and the spec depends on stream normalization, seam-level verification is missing.

The request-plumbing requirement only says metadata survives when a request passes through `RouterCompatAdapter` or `RpcProvider` (`specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md`), but the proposal impact includes `crates/clankers-provider/src/lib.rs`, `crates/clankers-provider/src/router.rs`, and `../clanker-router/src/provider.rs`. The napkin calls out multiple `CompletionRequest` types and an easy-to-miss construction site in `crates/clankers-provider/src/router.rs`. Current artifacts do not require `_session_id` and provider extras to exist on every request shape/construction site, so the stated capability can be satisfied narrowly while still dropping metadata elsewhere.

Discovery/model-resolution requirements are not traced to an explicit verification task. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requires both: (a) `openai-codex` appears when `openai-codex` creds exist, and (b) it stays hidden when they do not, while API-key `openai` behavior is unchanged. In `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/tasks.md`, task `3.1` only implements catalog/discovery, and task `3.5` lists tests for request mapping, SSE parsing, tool calls, reasoning signatures, session cache key propagation, and retry behavior, but not discovery visibility/model-resolution cases.

Task `4.2` weakens the design’s required real-credential smoke path. `design.md` says under Risks / Trade-offs: “Use unit + fixture coverage for parsing and streaming, then keep one manual smoke path for real credentials.” But `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/tasks.md` item `4.2` says: “Verify end-to-end flows ... using live credentials or recorded fixture data.” “or recorded fixture data” allows skipping the manual real-credential check the design called for, which matters for a private/beta endpoint.

The task list misses the repo’s required Nix validation after extracted dependency updates. `tasks.md` item `3.6` explicitly updates the extracted `clanker-router` dependency/pin, but `4.3` only runs `cargo nextest run` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`. The napkin entry dated 2026-04-11 says path-dependency repo bumps must also validate `nix build .#clankers`, and if Nix fails, check `crate-hashes.json` and `flake.nix` `externalSources`. This is a known deterministic failure mode tied directly to `3.6`.

The stream-mapping tasks do not require a real SSE seam test despite introducing a new SSE transport and stream normalization path. `tasks.md` items `3.2`–`3.5` add the Codex Responses SSE backend and event mapping, but `3.5` only says “fixture-backed unit and integration tests.” The napkin entry dated 2026-04-08 says stream rewriter changes need at least one test at the real seam `parse_sse_stream(..., reverse_map = true)`, not only helper/unit coverage. That gap is relevant to `specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requirement “Codex response streams normalize into generic stream events.”

The task list does not explicitly preserve or verify the “provider omitted => Anthropic-compatible default” behavior promised in the design. `design.md` Decision 6 says: “Default behavior stays Anthropic-compatible when provider is omitted.” But `tasks.md` item `1.3` only says auth handlers should “accept explicit provider names, show provider-scoped status, and reload the affected provider,” and `1.4` lists generic auth tests without calling out the omitted-provider regression case. That is a traceability gap against the stated UX contract.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/proposal.md` sets a backward-compatibility expectation in **Verification**: "Unit tests for provider-aware auth, account-ID derivation, refresh, switch, logout, and omitted-provider Anthropic defaults." But `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` only specifies explicit `--provider openai-codex` and `/login openai-codex` flows. There is no requirement or scenario for `clankers auth ...` or `/login` without a provider continuing to default to Anthropic. That leaves a regression boundary unencoded.

The metadata-plumbing spec does not fully encode the proposal's stated coverage requirement. `proposal.md` says verification must prove "`extra_params` and `_session_id` survive every `CompletionRequest` construction path that feeds the router`," but `specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` only gives one scenario: "WHEN the request passes through `RouterCompatAdapter` or `RpcProvider`". The supplied napkin also calls out a recurring miss: there are multiple `CompletionRequest` types and "Third `CompletionRequest` construction site in `clankers-provider/src/router.rs` (test module) — easy to miss." This should be hardened into an explicit spec rule/check over all router-bound construction paths, not left implicit.

`specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` only defines successful derivation of `chatgpt_account_id` from the access token's `https://api.openai.com/auth` claim, while `specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requires every `openai-codex` request header set to include `chatgpt-account-id`. No scenario covers a missing or malformed claim on initial login or refresh. The current artifacts do not say whether login fails, refresh invalidates the account, or requests surface an auth error when derivation is impossible.

Account-ID derivation is not specified consistently enough for task 1.2 to be implementable or verifiable. `proposal.md` says the `chatgpt-account-id` header is “extracted from a JWT claim,” `design.md` Decision 2 says to extract `https://api.openai.com/auth.chatgpt_account_id`, but `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` says to derive `chatgpt_account_id` from the access token’s `https://api.openai.com/auth` claim payload. This conflict needs resolution before the tasks stage can be considered complete.

The tasks do not explicitly cover provider-scoped pending OAuth state isolation, even though the spec requires it. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` scenario “CLI login starts an OpenAI Codex flow” requires storing provider-specific verifier/state “without overwriting Anthropic login state,” but `tasks.md` items 1.1-1.4 only mention a provider-aware driver abstraction, helpers, command updates, and general auth tests. There is no task or verification step that proves concurrent or sequential Anthropic and `openai-codex` login state cannot collide.

Streaming verification is underspecified relative to the design and spec. `design.md` Verification Plan requires a real SSE seam test validating “text, tool-use, reasoning, signature replay, usage, and stop reasons” where bytes enter the system, and `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` has separate scenarios for text stream ordering plus usage/stop-reason reporting. `tasks.md` item 3.6 includes fixture-backed tests and “at least one real SSE seam test,” but it only names request mapping, tool calls, reasoning signatures, session cache key propagation, and retry behavior. It does not explicitly require assertions for text event ordering, usage normalization, or stop-reason mapping.

Account-ID derivation is inconsistent across current artifacts. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` Decision 2 says the OpenAI Codex driver "extracts `https://api.openai.com/auth.chatgpt_account_id`" from the JWT, but `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` Scenario "Login derives the account identifier" says it derives `chatgpt_account_id` from the token's "`https://api.openai.com/auth` claim payload". Those are different parsing contracts. Resolve the authoritative token schema before implementation or tasks proceed.

The retry contract is not concrete enough for the added response-provider requirements. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requires bounded backoff for HTTP 429/5xx and exactly one refresh-and-retry on 401. In `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md`, the design only states the goal of "retry" and the verification plan mentions "reactive single refresh-and-retry on 401", but it does not define retry count, backoff bounds, or how 401 refresh interacts with transient retries. That leaves implementation and verification ambiguous.

This change depends on preserving `extra_params`/`_session_id` through every `CompletionRequest` construction path, and the existing artifacts acknowledge that risk, but the repo has a known recurring miss pattern here. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/proposal.md` Verification says these fields must survive "every `CompletionRequest` construction path that feeds the router", and `design.md` repeats coverage for local router adapter and RPC provider paths. Napkin guidance records multiple `CompletionRequest` types plus an easy-to-miss third construction site in `crates/clankers-provider/src/router.rs` (test module). Promote this into a deterministic audit/check or required enumerated test matrix instead of relying on informal coverage.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` includes the scenario **"Missing or malformed claim blocks Codex use"** and requires clankers to surface a provider-auth error and avoid sending Codex requests without a valid `chatgpt-account-id`. In `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/tasks.md`, task `1.2` only says **"Implement OpenAI Codex authorize, code-exchange, refresh, and account-ID derivation helpers"** and task `1.5` only mentions tests for **"account-ID derivation from refreshed tokens"**. No task explicitly covers malformed/missing-claim rejection or verification that request execution is blocked in that case.

The proposal says OpenAI Codex accounts must be **"authenticated, refreshed, listed, switched, and logged out"**, and `specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` has the scenario **"Switching or logout affects only the selected provider"**. In `tasks.md`, task `1.4` covers explicit provider names, status, Anthropic defaults, and reload-after-login, but does not explicitly call out provider-scoped switch/logout implementation. Only task `1.5` mentions tests for **"switching, logout"**. The implementation work is not explicitly traced.

The earlier artifacts require live verification to happen after automated coverage: `proposal.md` says **"One manual live-credential smoke path ... after fixture coverage passes"** and `design.md` says **"Manual verification covers one live-credential smoke path after automated coverage passes."** In `tasks.md`, `4.2 Run a manual live-credential smoke path...` is ordered before `4.3 Keep recorded-fixture end-to-end coverage...` and `4.4 Run cargo nextest ... clippy ... nix build .#clankers`. The task list does not preserve the stated dependency/order.

The initial `openai-codex` model catalog is not concretely specified. `design.md` Decision 8 says the curated catalog includes the supported Codex family “for example `gpt-5.1-codex`, `gpt-5.1-codex-max`, `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.3-codex`,” which leaves the actual in-scope model list unresolved. This blocks deterministic discovery/model-resolution tasks and tests for `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` Requirement “OpenAI Codex is discovered as a separate provider family.”

The request-plumbing coverage is still too abstract for a change whose core risk is dropping provider extras at one constructor seam. `design.md` Verification Plan says coverage includes “at minimum turn execution, helper request builders, local router adapter, and RPC provider paths,” but it does not enumerate the concrete construction sites implicated by the proposal impact (`crates/clankers-agent/src/turn/execution.rs`, `crates/clankers-provider/src/{lib.rs,router.rs,rpc_provider.rs}`) or the recurring napkin pattern about multiple `CompletionRequest` types and the easy-to-miss test construction site in `clankers-provider/src/router.rs`. Promote this into a deterministic check or hard spec rule that inventories every router-bound `CompletionRequest` construction site and verifies `extra_params` / `_session_id` preservation or explicit initialization.

Provider-aware account-management UX is not specified as concretely as the auth requirements require. The proposal says OpenAI Codex accounts must be “refreshed, listed, switched, and logged out alongside Anthropic,” and `specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` requires provider-scoped switch/logout behavior. But `design.md` Decision 7 only gives explicit UX examples for login (`clankers auth login --provider openai-codex`, `/login openai-codex`) and grouped status/account listings. It does not define the command contract for switch/logout, how provider selection is expressed there, or whether omitted-provider behavior defaults to Anthropic for those commands too.

The change does not define where `_session_id` is created or what makes it “stable.” `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/proposal.md` says the change will “carry provider-specific extra parameters and a stable session key into router backends” and lists `src/modes/interactive.rs` and `crates/clankers-agent/src/turn/execution.rs` in Impact, but `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` only requires preserving `_session_id` if present (“Session identifier survives adapter conversion”; “Every router-bound request path preserves or initializes extras”). There is no requirement for which component must generate it, whether it is per interactive session vs per conversation vs per request, or when it must be reused across later turns. That leaves core request/cache behavior under-specified for design.

Omitted-provider behavior is only specified for login, not for the rest of the newly provider-aware auth surface. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/proposal.md` says “Auth commands and slash commands become provider-aware instead of Anthropic-only” and Verification calls for tests covering “switch, logout, and omitted-provider Anthropic defaults.” In `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md`, the only default-provider scenario is “Omitted provider keeps the Anthropic default” under login. There is no matching requirement for `auth status`, `switch`, `logout`, or `/account` when provider is omitted, so backward-compatible CLI behavior is ambiguous.

The known multi-site `CompletionRequest` propagation footgun should not stay as prose-only guidance. `proposal.md` Verification and `specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` both require `extra_params` / `_session_id` to survive “every `CompletionRequest` construction path.” The supplied napkin already identifies the recurring miss points: provider `CompletionRequest`, router `CompletionRequest`, and the third construction site in `clankers-provider/src/router.rs` test code. This should be promoted into a deterministic check or explicit spec rule, not left to ad hoc test selection.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/tasks.md` task 2.1 says to "audit every `CompletionRequest { ... }` construction path" and 2.3 only tests pass-through, but no task adds the deterministic guard required by `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` Verification Plan: "The implementation adds a deterministic constructor-inventory check or audit test so new router-bound `CompletionRequest` call sites cannot omit `extra_params` silently." This also aligns with the napkin pattern that there are multiple `CompletionRequest` types and an easy-to-miss third construction site in `crates/clankers-provider/src/router.rs` test code. As written, the requirement in `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` that router-bound constructors are "audited as a complete set" is not enforceable.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requires `_session_id` reuse across multiple turns and says "a resumed session reuses that same value instead of generating a fresh per-request identifier." `tasks.md` 2.2 only says to thread stable session identifiers into `_session_id`, and 2.3 only says to test survival through local and RPC provider paths. No task explicitly verifies same-session reuse over later turns or resumed-session reuse, so a core session-continuity requirement is missing from verification.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` Decision 4 fixes the retry policy in detail: up to 3 retries for 429/retryable 5xx with 1s/2s/4s backoff, exactly one 401 refresh and one immediate retry, and never more than one refresh cycle per top-level request. `tasks.md` 3.4 only says "Implement transient retry and reactive 401 refresh for Codex requests," and 3.6 only says to test "retry behavior." The task text does not carry the current design constraints, so implementation and verification are ambiguous against the approved design.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` requires, in scenario **“CLI login starts an OpenAI Codex flow”**, that login stores “provider-specific verifier and state needed to finish login without overwriting Anthropic login state.” `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` Decision 2 introduces provider-aware OAuth drivers and PKCE generation, but it does not say how in-progress OAuth state is stored or keyed by provider/account. Without that isolation spelled out, the design does not fully cover the auth spec and leaves a requirement-critical behavior to implementation guesswork.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` Verification Plan says the implementation will add “a deterministic constructor-inventory check or audit test” for router-bound `CompletionRequest` sites. That is too weak and ambiguous for a known failure mode. `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` already makes this a complete-set audit requirement, and the napkin history calls out multiple `CompletionRequest` types plus an easy-to-miss construction site in `crates/clankers-provider/src/router.rs`. This should be hardened to a deterministic inventory check, not left as “or audit test.”

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` scenario **“Slash-command login uses the same provider flow”** requires that successful login “triggers provider credential reload without restarting the session.” `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/design.md` Decision 7 covers `/login openai-codex` UX, but it does not describe the reload seam for the running interactive session, and the Verification Plan’s auth bullet omits any slash/interactive reload test. This is a traceability gap for a SHALL behavior.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/proposal.md` names capability `openai-codex-model-selection` and lists impact on "Model catalog / registry entries for `openai-codex`", and the Why section says users currently "cannot select Codex models". But `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` only specifies internal discovery behavior: "the discovered provider list includes `openai-codex`" and "Codex models resolve to that provider instead of the API-key `openai` backend." It does not define the user-visible catalog entries, model identifiers, or how users distinguish/select Codex subscription models versus API-key `openai` models. That leaves the proposal's model-selection capability under-specified for design.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md`, Requirement "Request headers carry account and auth metadata", requires `OpenAI-Beta: responses=experimental`. The supplied napkin shows a recurring failure mode for experimental headers being added on some request paths but missed on others: "`prompt-caching-2024-07-31` beta header needed in ALL ... request paths". The proposal's Verification section has no deterministic guard for this. Add a deterministic check or explicit rule that every Codex Responses request path, including retries/alternate adapters, sets the required beta header.

`proposal.md` says verification must prove `extra_params` and `_session_id` survive “every `CompletionRequest` construction path that feeds the router,” and `specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` adds “Router-bound request constructors are audited as a complete set,” but neither artifact defines the concrete constructor inventory or a deterministic way to enforce that inventory. The affected paths are already hinted by `proposal.md` Impact (`crates/clankers-agent/src/turn/execution.rs`, `crates/clankers-provider/src/{lib.rs,router.rs,rpc_provider.rs}`, `../clanker-router/src/provider.rs`). Napkin history explicitly calls out multiple `CompletionRequest` types and an easy-to-miss test construction site. This should be hardened into an explicit check before design/implementation proceeds.

`proposal.md` Impact includes `../clanker-router/src/{auth.rs,oauth.rs,provider.rs}` and a new router backend module, while the change under review is `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans` in the current repo. The delta specs also require router-side behavior (`RouterCompatAdapter`, `RpcProvider`, retry/header handling, stream normalization). Evidence is insufficient whether this OpenSpec change governs that sibling repo or whether a coordinated external change/review is required. Repo-boundary ownership and validation responsibility are not stated.

`tasks.md` 1.3 regresses a design constraint. `design.md` Decision 2 says, “Pending OAuth verifier and state are also keyed by provider and account, not stored in one shared singleton slot.” But `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/tasks.md` 1.3 only says “Isolate pending OAuth verifier and state storage by provider so an `openai-codex` login cannot overwrite Anthropic in-flight login state.” That drops same-provider, different-account concurrency from scope. Add task text and verification for provider+account scoping, not provider-only scoping.

The beta-header verification requirement is underspecified at the tasks stage. `proposal.md` Verification requires “A deterministic request-fixture check that every Codex request path, including retries and refresh retries, sends `OpenAI-Beta: responses=experimental`.” `specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` also requires that retry and refresh follow-up requests keep the header. But `tasks.md` 3.6 only asks for tests of “required beta-header propagation on initial and retried requests,” which is not the same as a deterministic check and does not explicitly cover the 401 refresh-and-retry path. Given the known multi-path header failure pattern in the napkin, this needs an explicit deterministic check task.

`proposal.md` requires “A deterministic request-fixture check that every Codex request path, including retries and refresh retries, sends \`OpenAI-Beta: responses=experimental\`.” `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` also requires retry and refresh follow-up requests to keep that header. `design.md` only says streaming tests validate required beta headers “where bytes enter the system”; it does not preserve the proposal’s explicit all-request-path deterministic check across initial, transient-retry, and refresh-retry paths. This is a known recurring failure pattern from the napkin and should be hardened into a deterministic check.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` requires: “Missing or malformed claim blocks Codex use,” including surfacing a provider-auth error and not sending requests without a valid `chatgpt-account-id`. `design.md` Decision 2 describes how `chatgpt_account_id` is derived from the `https://api.openai.com/auth` claim, but it does not specify the failure behavior when that claim is missing/malformed, and `design.md` Verification Plan does not include a negative-path test for that scenario.

`design.md` Decision 2 says “Pending OAuth verifier and state are also keyed by provider and account,” but the same decision says the `openai-codex` account ID is only derived after code exchange or refresh from the access token. That conflicts with `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` scenario “CLI login starts an OpenAI Codex flow,” where no account exists yet. The design needs one coherent keying strategy for in-progress login state before account derivation.

`openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md` requires non-retryable 4xx failures other than 401 to surface directly without retry. `design.md` Decision 4 implies that behavior by enumerating retryable cases, but `design.md` Verification Plan only names reactive 401 refresh-and-retry and transient retries; it does not include an explicit no-retry verification for non-401 4xx responses.

Tasks do not explicitly cover re-deriving `chatgpt_account_id` when persisted `openai-codex` credentials are loaded or reloaded. `design.md` says, “the in-memory credential manager re-derives the account ID whenever credentials are loaded or refreshed,” but `tasks.md` only names derivation in `1.2` (“authorize, code-exchange, refresh, account-ID derivation...”) and tests in `1.5` include “account-ID derivation from refreshed tokens,” not load/reload/startup derivation from persisted credentials. This is a traceability gap against the chosen design seam.

Proactive/background refresh is required by spec but not called out as its own implementation/verification task. `specs/openai-codex-auth/spec.md` requires “OpenAI Codex credentials refresh automatically” and its scenario says, “WHEN background credential refresh runs THEN the system exchanges the refresh token... AND updates the persisted auth store entry.” In `tasks.md`, `1.2` implements refresh helpers, `1.5` mentions token refresh tests, and `3.4` covers reactive `401` refresh-and-retry, but no task explicitly wires `openai-codex` into the background refresh path or verifies that specific path.

`tasks.md` item `4.2` is phrased as “Keep recorded-fixture end-to-end coverage...” rather than a concrete add/update action. That makes completion less deterministic than neighboring items such as `3.6` and `3.7`, which explicitly require added tests/checks.

The delta specs do not encode the proposal’s core constraint that API-key `openai` behavior must remain unchanged. Evidence: `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/proposal.md` says “Add discovery and model catalog entries for `openai-codex`, while keeping API-key `openai` behavior unchanged” and lists “Replacing API-key `openai` with the Responses API” under **Non-Goals**. In `openspec/changes/support-openai-subscription-plans/specs/openai-codex-responses/spec.md`, requirements cover discovery separation and require that `openai-codex` use the Codex Responses transport, but there is no corresponding requirement that provider `openai` continue using its existing API-key path and not inherit Codex/OAuth behavior.
